title: Help Species at Risk – Protect Nature Challenge - Canada.ca
content:
## Help Species at Risk – Protect Nature Challenge
Human activity is placing wildlife in peril at a faster rate than ever before.
More than one million species are now facing extinction globally, including more than 600 at-risk species in Canada.
Want to help? Here are 10 things you can do to help species at risk:
- Learn about species at risk in Canada and visit places that protect these species, like parks, zoos and botanical gardens.
- Volunteer with organizations who help recover species at risk in your area.
- Install bird feeders.
- Grow native plants in your garden.
- Question residential area plans that could destroy the habitats of species at risk.
- Help reduce climate change by walking, biking and taking public transportation.
- Do not use unnecessary pesticides around the home.
- When travelling, remember that it is sometimes illegal to bring back, without a permit, souvenirs made from plants and animals.
- Consume less and buy from companies that are involved in protecting the environment.
- Respect laws and regulations regarding species at risk.

## Let’s do this together
Share these challenges with your friends and family and show us what you are doing by tagging us in your social media messages and by using the hashtag #ProtectNature.
We’re on Instagram @canenvironment , Facebook @EnvironmentandClimateChange and Twitter @environmentca .
Check out our list of challenges to help protect nature.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
